name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-52
title: rabbit-config.py human-approval 'false' message uses 'DISABLED'/'ENABLED' wording (confusing semantics)
description: skills/rabbit-config/scripts/rabbit-config.py cmd_human_approval prints messages like 'Human-approval gate DISABLED (false). Marker written: ...' and 'Human-approval gate ENABLED (true). Marker removed: ...'. The mapping is: true=gate-active (enabled-as-protection), false=gate-bypassed (disabled-as-protection). The DISABLED-when-false / ENABLED-when-true terminology is correct but confusing because the CLI argument and the description's adjective both invert semantically. The spec/SKILL.md uses 'gate active' / 'gate bypassed' or 'gate active' / 'gate disabled'. Recommend the script message use the same vocabulary as spec.md to avoid confusion ('Step 4 gate is now ACTIVE (true)' / 'Step 4 gate is now BYPASSED (false)').

history.note: Fixed Wave 3: BUG-29 new-feature.py scaffolds .py runner + template_version; BUG-31 rabbit-project.md uses real .py paths; BUG-52 human-approval messages name marker+effect.
